questions.text: I believe that indirectness in communication wastes everyone's time.
I believe that every hour I spend on myself is an hour I could have spent helping someone.
I feel that a well-directed team outperforms a democratic one.
I find that I can process upsetting news and continue functioning without much disruption.
I hold the view that the moral priority of suffering is a truth that becomes more obvious, not less, with experience.
I feel a genuine sense of accomplishment when I execute a plan exactly as designed.
I hold the view that the moral demand of suffering is clearest when I understand not just the fact of it but the full human context surrounding it.
I think of myself as someone who drives outcomes.
I am quick to notice when someone's status in a group has shifted.
I can usually tell when someone is flirting versus just being friendly.
I believe that a calm, analytical approach to human pain is not coldness but a higher form of engagement with the problem.
I find it difficult to dial down my emotional intensity once it has ramped up.
I prioritize tasks based on urgency and importance using a deliberate framework.
I tend to define a good life as one spent in generous contribution to others.
I feel that even positive experiences can be overwhelming when they arrive with full intensity.
I prefer to move fast and fix errors when they surface rather than reviewing obsessively.
I am energized by arriving at a decision that everyone shaped.
I believe that personal mastery is one of the most rewarding pursuits in life.
